steps.title: Identify the key pattern the simulation tests
steps.content: The passage establishes a core finding: plant species with narrow geographical ranges are genetically homogeneous, while those with extensive ranges are less so. The simulation examines what would happen if M. klissingiana expanded from its narrow range (Tamaulipas only) to new habitats — a shift from narrow to extensive range.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the outcome
steps.content:
When a species expands from narrow to extensive range, the established pattern predicts genetic homogeneity should decrease. Choice B captures this exactly: genetic homogeneity of M. klissingiana decreased. The other three discuss other species or locations, not the genetic change in M. klissingiana when it spreads.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
